title: Configuring loggers is security-sensitive
description: Configuring loggers is security-sensitive. It has led in the past to the following vulnerabilities: Logs are useful before, during and after a security incident. Attackers will most of the time start their nefarious work by probing the system for vulnerabilities.
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects logger configuration calls including AddLogging, ConfigureLogging, LoggerFactory constructors, and ILoggerFactory extension methods (AddConsole, AddDebug, etc.)
logic.triggering_conditions: Triggers when logger configuration methods are called OR LoggerFactory objects are instantiated, excluding test code and development-only contexts
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes test classes, test methods, conditional DEBUG compilation blocks, and normal logger usage (LogInformation, LogError, etc.)
never_trigger_on: Normal logging calls (LogInformation, LogError, LogWarning, LogDebug)
logic.reasoning: Logger configuration is security-sensitive as improper configuration can leak sensitive data or miss critical security events. The rule targets configuration methods specifically, not normal logging usage. Test code is excluded since logging configuration in tests is expected. We check for specific configuration method names (AddLogging, ConfigureLogging) and LoggerFactory creation, while excluding common logger usage patterns. Uses both CallExpression and Assignment nodes since some configurations are detected as assignments.
